title: Classic Storage Bed (Queen)
summary: A queen bed built from three plywood cabinet benches side by side — no headboard, no frame rails, just cabinetry that holds a mattress, with optional drawers in each bay.
description:
This bed isn't built like a bed at all — it's three separate plywood cabinet carcasses, built identically, arranged in a row on the floor. There's no headboard, no siderail, no frame in the usual sense; the cabinets ARE the frame, and the mattress simply sits on top of all three. That's what makes the storage genuinely generous compared to a bed with under-bed drawers bolted to a frame: every cubic inch inside all three benches is usable, because the benches were designed as storage first and a bed base second.

Build all three benches identically through the carcass and shelf steps — the only bench that's actually different is the one at either end of the row, which gets an extra face frame on its exposed outer side (the two side-by-side benches only show their fronts; the end bench shows a front AND a side). Keep track of which bench is your end bench before you get to that step, or you'll trim out the wrong one.

A center support of scrap plywood strips bridges the gap in the middle of the bed where the three benches meet, since that's the one place a mattress spans open air instead of solid cabinet top — screw it down rather than glue it, since the whole point of building a bed from separable units is being able to break it back down later.

Drawers are entirely optional and can be added now or built later without touching anything already finished — each bench's cubby stands on its own as open storage if you'd rather skip the drawer boxes and slides. If you do build them: measure YOUR actual openings and drawer slides before cutting box parts, since a plywood carcass built by hand rarely lands on the exact plan dimension, and a drawer box needs to match the real opening and the real slide hardware, not the number on this page.

tools.note: Ripping the plywood carcass panels and the scrap center-support strips.
Measure your actual openings and drawer slides before cutting any optional drawer part.

materials.name: 3/4" cabinet-grade plywood, MDF, or AC plywood, 4x8 sheet
materials.note: Cabinet-grade plywood for a stained finish; MDF or AC plywood work for paint, at lower cost and more sanding.

materials.name: 1x6, 8 ft (optional drawers)
materials.note: Drawer box sides, fronts, and backs.

materials.name: Drawer slides, 18"
materials.note: Optional — six drawers total across the three benches.

materials.name: Self-tapping screws, 1-1/2"
materials.note: Assembling the center support and joining the benches, if desired.

cutList.note: End bench only — the one bench whose side actually shows.
Bridges the gap where the three benches meet in the middle of the bed.
Optional — sides always match your drawer slide length.
Optional — adjust to fit your actual openings and slides.
Optional — cut to fit your assembled drawer box.

steps.title: Build all three bench carcasses
steps.body:
Attach the bench ends and one divider to the bench top with 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws, three per joint, placed about an inch from each edge. Use one of the shelf boards from the next step as a spacer to center the divider perfectly.

Build all three benches this way before moving on to the next step.
materials: 3/4" cabinet-grade plywood, MDF, or AC plywood, 4x8 sheet

steps.title: Install the shelves
steps.body:
Drill 3/4" pocket holes on each end of the shelf boards and attach them to the sides and divider of every bench. Scrap wood blocks cut to the shelf's height make handy spacers while you position and screw each one in.

Continue for all three benches.
materials: 3/4" cabinet-grade plywood, MDF, or AC plywood, 4x8 sheet

steps.title: Attach the backs
steps.body:
Nail and glue the 1/4" plywood back to each of the three benches.

Continue for all three benches.

steps.title: Build and attach the face frames
steps.body:
Cut the 1x2 and 1x4 face-frame pieces with a miter saw and build each face frame flat, on a bench, rather than in place. Nail the completed face frames to all three benches.

For whichever bench sits at the end of the row (the only one whose SIDE will actually show), build two additional face frames sized to trim out that exposed end, and nail and glue them on now — don't wait until the benches are arranged in the room to remember which one needs it.

steps.title: Trim the side benches' backs and set the benches in the room
steps.body:
On the two SIDE benches only (not the end bench), attach a 1x4 cleat to the back, held down 3/4" from the top of the bed, nailed into the divider and end backs with glue.

Arrange the three benches side by side in the room. Screw them together if you'd like the assembly to stay put, then lay scrap plywood (random sizes are fine) across the opening in the center of the bed where the three benches meet. Screw the scrap down rather than glue it — that keeps the whole bed easy to take apart later if it ever needs to move.

steps.title: Build the optional drawers
steps.body:
Mark a line on each 1x4 cleat 1" up from its bottom edge. Attach the drawer slide's cabinet member to the cleat, front edge flush, with the slide's mounting holes lined up on your pencil mark — that keeps every slide level. Remember the opposing slide in each pair installs as a mirror image.

Set the cleats inside the cubbies, front edges flush with the plywood carcass fronts, and nail to the sides with 1-1/4" brad nails.

Build the drawer boxes from 1x6, with the sides always matching your slide length exactly — adjust the front and back length to fit YOUR actual openings and slides rather than the plan number. Join with 3/4" pocket holes on the outsides (hidden later by the drawer face) and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws. Nail the 1/4" plywood bottom on with glue and 3/4" brad nails, then install the drawer-member slides and fit the drawers in place.

steps.title: Fit the drawer faces
steps.body:
Measure and cut the drawer faces to fit, trimming down on a table saw if needed. Nail each face to the front of its drawer box in a few spots with an even gap on all four sides, avoiding anywhere hardware will be mounted.

Remove the drawer and further secure the face with 1-1/4" screws driven from inside the drawer box, again avoiding the hardware locations.

images.alt: Queen storage bed built from three side-by-side plywood cabinet benches with optional drawers
